FNFrenchie Now
HomeDNABreedingMarketScores
Premium Genetics Platform

Frenchie Now

Professional-grade DNA, breeding probabilities, and market intelligence for the world's most sought-after French Bulldogs.

DNA Visualizer

Interactive genotype diagrams for every Frenchie color and pattern.

Breeding Calculator

Select two parents and see offspring probabilities with Punnett squares.

Market Data

Price charts, rarity distribution, and demand/supply analytics.

Recommendations

Ranked purchase suggestions based on ROI, health, and rarity.

Ethical Breeding Reminder

This tool is for education and responsible breeding decisions. Never breed double-merle (MM) pairings. Always verify parent health tests and genetic screening before breeding.

All Frenchie Types

18 Types
common

Brindle

Classic striped pattern. The most common and historically standard Frenchie color.

common

Fawn

Light tan/golden color with a black mask. The second most common color.

uncommon

Cream

Pale ivory to off-white. Recessive red (e/e) overrides other pigments.

rare

Blue

Black pigment diluted to a blue/gray. dd genotype.

rare

Chocolate

Rich brown pigment. b/b genotype. Often with yellow/green eyes.

rare

Lilac

bb + dd combined. A pale silvery-brown. Highly sought after.

very rare

Isabella

bb + dd + coco. An even rarer pale champagne-lilac shade.

very rare

New Shade Isabella

The rarest shade — almost pinkish-lavender with additional modifiers.

uncommon

Solid Black

Solid black without brindle. K/K or K/kbr dominant black.

rare

Blue & Tan

dd + at/at. Blue body with tan points on cheeks, chest, legs.

rare

Chocolate & Tan

bb + at/at. Chocolate with tan points. Very popular.

very rare

Lilac & Tan

bb + dd + at/at. Lilac with tan points. Premium combination.

very rare

Blue Merle

dd + Mm. Marbled blue/gray pattern. Mm only — never MM.

very rare

Chocolate Merle

bb + Mm. Marbled chocolate pattern.

ultra rare

Lilac Merle

bb + dd + Mm. Marbled lilac pattern. One of the rarest patterns.

uncommon

Pied

White patches on colored body. sp/sp or S/sp genotype.

ultra rare

Fluffy

Long, soft coat. Lh/Lh recessive. Can appear in any color.

very rare

Platinum

Extreme dilution near-white. Often dd + ee or heavy dilute modifiers.
